expectations.evidence: Code uses role="alertdialog" (which is a more specific type of dialog) and aria-modal="true" on the dialog div element

expectations.text: Response implements focus trapping within the modal
expectations.evidence: handleKeyDown callback implements Tab/Shift+Tab cycling: queries all focusable elements inside dialogRef, wraps focus from last to first element on Tab and first to last on Shift+Tab using e.preventDefault()

expectations.text: Response adds Escape key handler to close
expectations.evidence: handleKeyDown includes: if (e.key === 'Escape') { e.stopPropagation(); onCancel(); return; }

expectations.text: Response saves and restores focus to triggering element
expectations.evidence: useEffect saves trigger: triggerRef.current = document.activeElement as HTMLElement when open=true, and restores: triggerRef.current?.focus() when open=false

expectations.text: Response uses role=alertdialog for destructive action
expectations.evidence: Code uses role="alertdialog" with explanation: 'Alert dialogs tell assistive technology that the user must acknowledge the content before continuing. Since this is a delete confirmation, alertdialog is the correct role.'
